text: Graph Neural Networks (GNNs) have been widely used in modeling graph-structured data for recommender systems. However, the real-world recommendation scenarios are often accompanied by heterogeneous relationships (e.g., social-aware user influence, knowledge-aware item dependency) that contain abundant and valuable information for user preference learning. Therefore, the heterogeneous graph-enhanced relational learning for recommendation is particularly meaningful and worth studying. In addition, contrastive self-supervised learning has achieved great success in recommendation. Hence, this work proposes a Heterogeneous Graph Contrastive Learning (HGCL) framework, which leverages heterogeneous relational semantics and applies contrastive learning-enhanced knowledge transfer between different views to better model user-item interactions. While the effects of heterogeneous side information on user-item interactions are different for users and items, this phenomenon should be reflected in the model. To this end, we further improve the heterogeneous graph contrastive learning by introducing a meta network to realize a personalized knowledge transformer with adaptive contrastive augmentation. Experimental results on three real-world datasets show that our proposed HGCL outperforms state-of-the-art baselines, confirming the validity of our design. The effectiveness of the key components in HGCL is further demonstrated through the ablation study. The source code of the model implementation is available at the link https://github.com/HKUDS/HGCL.
